title: 七年级(下)英语学科期末自测卷

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 A
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料,从每题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
June 7th Saturday Cloudy
What an exciting weekend I had! We had a basketball game with the team Center City. The players in that team were all tall and strong. They thought they could beat(战胜) us. But we played well. At last, we won the game by 4:3.
—Eric

June 14th Saturday Rainy
My friend Dale phoned me to go to the beach this morning. But it started to rain after breakfast. I just watched the boring TV show at home.
—Jim

June 15th Sunday Sunny
I had a busy Sunday. In the morning, I went to the art club to learn to draw for two hours. Then I cleaned the rooms. In the afternoon, I cooked dinner with my mother for my father. Do you know why? It was Father's Day! In the evening, my parents and I went to see a movie.
—Ruth

questions.prompt: When did Eric have the basketball game?
questions.translation: 埃里克什么时候打的篮球赛？
questions.explanation: 根据日志首句 'June 7th Saturday Cloudy' 以及 'We had a basketball game...' 可知，篮球赛是在6月7日进行的。

questions.prompt: Jim couldn't go to the beach because ________.
options: he had to watch the basketball game
he had to learn to draw
he cleaned the rooms at home
it started to rain after breakfast
questions.translation: 吉姆不能去海滩是因为________。
questions.explanation: 根据第二篇日志中 'But it started to rain after breakfast' 可知，早饭后开始下雨导致他没能去成海滩。

questions.prompt: What did Ruth think of her Sunday?
questions.translation: 鲁思觉得她的星期天怎么样？
questions.explanation: 根据第三篇日志首句 'I had a busy Sunday.' 可知，她觉得自己的周日很忙碌。

questions.prompt: Which one is TRUE?
options: It was sunny when Eric had the basketball game.
Father's Day is on June 15th this year.
Jim learned to draw and cleaned the rooms on Sunday morning.
Ruth went to see a movie with Jim and Eric.
questions.translation: 哪一个是正确的？
questions.explanation: 根据第三篇日志 'June 15th Sunday... It was Father's Day!' 可知，那一年的父亲节是在6月15日。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 B
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料,从每题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
The Silk Road(丝绸之路) was once a route(路线) between China and Europe. Xinjiang covers over half of the route. Let's go along the route and find out some beautiful places.
Kashi(喀什)
First, let's go to Kashi by plane. It is the window of Xinjiang. It borders(接壤) on Tajikistan, Afghanistan and Pakistan. China plans to build a railway from Kashi to Pakistan.
Kuqa(库车)
Then we'll take the high-speed train to Kuqa. It is a land with beautiful singing and dancing. Kuqa is famous for a special musical instrument. People usually play the lute (琉特琴, 诗琴). Its dance is also very famous. Dancers use almost every part of their bodies, especially the waist and the neck.
Hami(哈密)
At last, we'll ride the camel to Hami. It is an important town in Xinjiang. You may know it because of the Hami melon. In fact, Xinjiang is home to many popular fruits, such as grapes in Turpan and pears in Kuerle.

questions.prompt: The Silk Road was once a route between China and ________.
questions.translation: 丝绸之路曾经是中国和________之间的一条路线。
questions.explanation: 根据文章第一句 'The Silk Road was once a route between China and Europe.' 可知正确答案为欧洲。

questions.prompt: Which is NOT RIGHT according to the description of Kashi?
options: The writer plans to fly to Kashi.
Kashi is the window of Xinjiang.
There is a railway from Kashi to Pakistan.
Tajikistan, Afghanistan and Pakistan are China's neighboring countries.
questions.translation: 根据对喀什的描述，哪一项是不正确的？
questions.explanation: 文章提到 'China plans to build a railway...'，即中国“计划”修建一条从喀什到巴基斯坦的铁路，而不是已经有了一条铁路。所以C是不正确的。

questions.prompt: What's the most popular fruit in Turpan?
questions.translation: 吐鲁番最受欢迎的水果是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据文末 'such as grapes in Turpan' 可知吐鲁番以葡萄闻名。

questions.prompt: If you plan to go to Hami for traveling from Kuqa, what's the writer's suggestion?
questions.translation: 如果你打算从库车去哈密旅游，作者的建议是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据Hami部分的开头 'At last, we'll ride the camel to Hami.' 可知建议是骑骆驼。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 C
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料,从每题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
Wang Wei was a famous poet and painter from the Tang Dynasty. His poetry and painting styles influenced (影响) each other. "In Mojie's poems, you can see a painting; in his paintings, you can feel a poem," said the Song Dynasty poet Su Shi.
Wang liked to paint people and natural scenes. He was the founder(创始人) of the Southern School of Chinese Landscape Painting. This school is known for its strong brushstrokes(笔触) and light ink washes.
About 400 of Wang's poems still exist and 29 of them are included in the poetry collection Three Hundred Tang Poems. Wang was good at writing jueju (绝句) poems. In the space of four lines, his jueju poems often describe peaceful mountains and rivers. The Deer Enclosure (《鹿柴》) is a good example:
In pathless hills no man's in sight,
But I still hear echoing sound.
In gloomy forest peeps no light,
But Sunbeams slant on mossy ground.
Wang Wei is remembered as one of the greatest poets and artists in Chinese history. His works continue to be read and loved to this day.

questions.prompt: What did Su Shi think of Wang Wei's works?
options: Wang Wei's poems were better than his paintings.
Wang Wei's paintings were better than his poems.
Wang Wei's poetry and painting styles influenced each other.
Wang Wei's works were not so great.
questions.translation: 苏轼对王维的作品有什么看法？
questions.explanation: 苏轼曾说“诗中有画，画中有诗”，对应文中“His poetry and painting styles influenced each other”即诗歌和绘画风格相互影响。

questions.prompt: What does the poem The Deer Enclosure mainly describe?
questions.translation: 《鹿柴》这首诗主要描写了什么？
questions.explanation: 文中指出 'his jueju poems often describe peaceful mountains and rivers. The Deer Enclosure is a good example'，所以是描写宁静的山峦。

questions.prompt: According to the text, which of the following statements is correct about Wang Wei's poems?
options: All of his poems are about people.
Less than 29 of his poems remain.
None of his poems are jueju poems.
His poems are full of artistic value.
questions.translation: 根据课文，关于王维的诗，下列哪项陈述是正确的？
questions.explanation: 王维作为中国历史上最伟大的诗人和艺术家之一，其作品具有极高的艺术价值（诗中有画）。选项A、B、C都与原文明显不符（现存约400首，29首收录于唐诗三百首，擅长写绝句）。

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of this text?
options: An introduction to the poem The Deer Enclosure.
The influence of the Southern School of Chinese Landscape Painting.
The friendship between Wang Wei and Su Shi.
An introduction to the famous poet and painter Wang Wei.
questions.translation: 这篇文章的中心思想是什么？
questions.explanation: 通篇围绕唐代著名的诗人和画家王维进行生平、风格、作品的介绍，故选D。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第一节 D
sections.instruction: 阅读下面语言材料,从每题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中选出最佳选项。
sections.passage:
[VISUAL: Black and white photograph of a young girl lying down next to a cat, gently touching its face.]
People usually think that cats are not very friendly. Unlike dogs, cats play with people less than dogs. They like to stay alone for hours. However, a new study shows that cats like people just as much as dogs.
Scientists at Oregon State University, the US, studied cats' relationships(关系) with their owners. They invited 117 cat owners to take a test. In the six-minute test, cat owners walked into a new room with their pets. After two minutes, the owners left the room, leaving the cats alone. When the owners came back two minutes later, the scientists looked at what the cats would do. About two-thirds of the cats came to greet the owners right away when they came back. The cats then went back to looking around the room, but still came back to their owners now and then.
"These cats," the scientists said, "saw their owners as a source of security(安全的来源) in a new place." In the same test, 58% of dogs acted just like the cats. "This idea that cats like to stay alone or don't really care about people isn't holding up," said Kristyn Vitale, a scientist who worked on the study. "The more we find out about cats, the more we see that they're social animals."

questions.prompt: People usually think that cats ________.
options: are likely to stay by themselves
like people as much as dogs
questions.translation: 人们通常认为猫________。
questions.explanation: 根据第一段 'They like to stay alone for hours.' 可知，人们常认为猫喜欢独处。

questions.prompt: Scientists made the test to find out ________.
options: the main difference between cats and dogs
the reasons why cats don't care about people
the relationship between cats and their owners
the ways that cat owners take care of their pets
questions.translation: 科学家们做这个测试是为了找出________。
questions.explanation: 根据第二段首句 '...studied cats' relationships with their owners.' 可知，测试的目的是研究猫与主人的关系。

questions.prompt: The underlined phrase "holding up" in the last paragraph probably means "________".
questions.translation: 最后一段中画线短语 “holding up” 概括的意思可能是 “________”。
questions.explanation: 根据科学家的研究发现，猫其实很依赖主人，因此原先人们认为的“猫喜欢独处或不在乎人”的观点是站不住脚的、不成立的（not true）。

questions.prompt: What's the best title for the passage?
questions.translation: 这篇文章最好的标题是什么？
questions.explanation: 文章推翻了猫冷漠的刻板印象，证明猫像狗一样把主人当成安全感来源，也是社交动物、人类的朋友。故选D。

sections.title: 一、阅读理解 第二节
sections.instruction: 阅读短文,从方框内所给的选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项,其中有一个多余的选项。
sections.passage:
Last weekend our geography teacher, Mr. Read, took us to Greentown on a school trip. He wrote letters to some hotels there. In the end he chose Ally Hotel. We had a terrible trip!
[1] Mr. Read drove the school bus. The journey took more than four hours! Some of us were asleep on the way because we got up very early in the morning. We finally arrived in Greentown at nearly 11:30 a.m. Then it started to rain heavily.
On Sunday morning, when we went downstairs for breakfast, we got a surprise. [2] Then at lunchtime, one of the boys fell into the river. Luckily, Mr. Read was very good at swimming. He heard his shouts and ran out to save him.
The Sunday afternoon was terrible, too. Two girls didn't tell Mr. Read and went to the center of the town. They got lost. A policeman brought them back to our hotel. [3] We returned to our city on Sunday evening. [4] Never again!
wordbank: We were cold, wet and tired.
There was water everywhere in the kitchen.
We left home at 7:00 on Saturday morning.
Mr. Read shouted at them because he was very angry.
We got up early on Sunday morning and looked outside.

questions.answer: We left home at 7:00 on Saturday morning.
questions.translation: 空格[1]选填项
questions.explanation: 后文提到“The journey took more than four hours! We finally arrived... at nearly 11:30 a.m.” 并且 “we got up very early”。C选项“我们星期六早上7:00离开家”完美顺承了出发的时间线。

questions.answer: There was water everywhere in the kitchen.
questions.translation: 空格[2]选填项
questions.explanation: 空格前说“when we went downstairs for breakfast, we got a surprise”，表明在下楼吃早餐时看到或发生的一件令人惊讶的事情。B选项“厨房里到处都是水”是一个惊吓/惊喜（surprise）。

questions.answer: Mr. Read shouted at them because he was very angry.
questions.translation: 空格[3]选填项
questions.explanation: 空格前提到“两个女孩没告诉老师就去了市中心结果迷路了。警察把她们送回了酒店”。紧接着应该是老师对她们的行为的反应，D选项“Mr. Read对她们大喊，因为他非常生气”符合常理。

questions.answer: We were cold, wet and tired.
questions.translation: 空格[4]选填项
questions.explanation: 最后总结这次糟糕的旅行，并说“Never again!（再也不去了！）”，A选项“我们又冷、又湿、又累”最能概括他们当时回城时的悲惨状态。

sections.title: 二、完形填空
sections.instruction: 阅读短文,理解其大意,从A、B、C、D四个选项中选出可以填入空白处的最佳选项。
sections.passage:
My family had a cat for years, and we all loved her. But one day, a(n) [1] came to my house as a new pet. The little dog was [2] and everyone in my family wanted to play with him. [3] the cat didn't like him, because she didn't want to live with [4] pet in the house. But the dog loved her and [5] her all the time, trying to play with her.
One day, a lynx (猞猁) [6] the cat, and the lynx planned to have the cat for lunch. When the little dog saw this, he ran towards the lynx quickly, [7] any hesitation(犹豫). The dog was much [8] than the dangerous animal. He did everything he could [9] his friend. The children happened to see him, so after that they gave the dog a new name—"Hero Dog".
From then on, the cat began to be friendly with the dog. [10] followed the little dog everywhere and even let the dog eat from her bowl. It was clear that the cat saw the little dog as a hero dog.

questions.translation: 一只小狗作为新宠物来到了我家。
questions.explanation: 根据后一句 'The little dog was...' 可知来的是一只狗。

questions.translation: 这只小狗非常可爱。
questions.explanation: 根据后半句 'everyone in my family wanted to play with him' 可知小狗十分招人喜欢、可爱。

questions.translation: 然而，猫并不喜欢他。
questions.explanation: 前面说全家都喜欢狗，后面说猫不喜欢，前后构成转折关系，用However。

questions.translation: 因为她不想和家里的另一只宠物一起生活。
questions.explanation: 表示在已有一只（猫）的情况下，另外“再一，又一”用another。

questions.translation: 但小狗很喜欢她，总是跟着她。
questions.explanation: 根据后文 'trying to play with her'，跟着她是为了和她一起玩。

questions.translation: 一天，一只猞猁抓住了这只猫。
questions.explanation: 猞猁计划把猫当午餐，所以是抓住了（caught）猫。

questions.translation: 毫不犹豫地冲向猞猁。
questions.explanation: without any hesitation 固定搭配，意为“毫不犹豫”。

questions.translation: 小狗比那个危险的动物要小得多。
questions.explanation: 根据than可知使用比较级，且狗通常比猞猁体型小。

questions.translation: 他尽其所能去救他的朋友。
questions.explanation: did everything he could to do sth. 这里的could后省略了do，后面接动词不定式表示目的。

questions.translation: 她开始到处跟着小狗。
questions.explanation: 这里的主语是指代前文的the cat（母猫，前面用了her），所以用She。

sections.title: 三、语篇填空
sections.instruction: 根据规则，现已将原本需要手工录入的语篇填空题转换为【下拉菜单单项选择题】，请从给出的选项中选出最契合上下文的正确答案形式。
sections.passage:
Many people go to see the world by themselves. They have big bags [1] the backs. They follow maps on [2] phones. They are called backpackers(背包客).
Before [3] for a new place, backpackers would like [4] their destinations(目的地). For example, what food to eat, where to live, what to buy and how to take trains. Most backpackers usually don't take too much money with them, they choose to stay in hostels (青年旅舍) during their trips. Hostels are much cheaper than big hotels. There [5] six or eight beds in each room. People [6] different countries become friends there.
Backpacking [7] travelers see more. An [8] backpacker Charles Veley went to over 190 [9] in 10 years! "To live is to see [10] world as much as possible," he said.

questions.translation: 他们在背上背着大包。
questions.explanation: 固定搭配，on the backs 在他们的背上。

questions.translation: 他们紧随手机上的地图。
questions.explanation: 修饰后面的名词phones，应该用形容词性物主代词their。

questions.translation: 在动身前往一个新地方之前。
questions.explanation: Before是介词，其后接动词时应使用动名词形式 leaving。

questions.translation: 背包客们想要研究他们的目的地。
questions.explanation: 固定搭配，would like to do sth. 想要做某事。

questions.translation: 每个房间里有六到八张床。
questions.explanation: There be 句型，主语是六到八张床（复数），时态为一般现在时，故用are。

questions.translation: 来自不同国家的人在那里成为了朋友。
questions.explanation: people from different countries，介词from表示“来自”。

questions.translation: 背包游让旅行者见识得更多。
questions.explanation: Backpacking动名词短语作主语，谓语动词应该用第三人称单数形式lets。

questions.translation: 一个美国背包客。
questions.explanation: 修饰名词backpacker，表示国家籍贯应该使用形容词 American。

questions.translation: 在10年里去了超过190个国家！
questions.explanation: 190修饰可数名词，名词要变成复数形式，country的复数形式是countries。

questions.translation: 去尽可能多地看看这个世界。
questions.explanation: 独一无二的事物（世界）前要加定冠词the，see the world。

sections.title: 四、阅读与表达
sections.instruction: 根据短文内容，完成下列各题（根据规则，前三题简答已改造为最佳单选项，第四题主观题升级为多项备选包容性考核模式）。
sections.passage:
Robbie looked out of the window. It was still snowing heavily and there was ice on the window. The family told Robbie to turn the heat on before they got home. And he did it. Then Robbie was told to do some cleaning work at once. It was an easy job for him. He kept on working until every room was clean and tidy.
Then, he had to cook dinner for the family. The first thing Robbie did was to get a big pot in the kitchen. Then he pour some water in the pot and put it on the stove (炉子). He used one of his hands to cut up a chicken and added the pieces to the water to make a good soup. Then he got some tomatoes, cabbages and carrots to make a vegetable salad.
At ten past eight he laid the table. Then he put some bread, the chicken soup and the salad on it. What a sweet smell! The moment he turned on the lights, the whole family came home.
"The soup smells great," said Helen. "You really did a good job." Robbie is one robot that really saves the family a lot of work.

questions.prompt: What was the weather like when the story happened?
options: It snowed heavily and there was ice on the window.
questions.translation: 故事发生时天气怎么样？
questions.explanation: 根据第一段首两句 'It was still snowing heavily and there was ice on the window.' 直接选出答案。

questions.prompt: What did Robbie do for the family in total (一共)?
options: He only turned on the heat and cooked dinner.
He just did some cleaning work.
He turned the heat on, did cleaning work, cooked dinner, and laid the table.
He only welcomed the family at the door.
questions.translation: 罗比总共为全家做了什么？
questions.explanation: 通读全身可知Robbie做了开暖气（turn the heat on）、打扫卫生（cleaning work）、做晚饭（cook dinner）和摆放餐桌餐具（laid the table）。

questions.prompt: What is Robbie?
questions.translation: 罗比是什么？
questions.explanation: 根据最后一句 'Robbie is one robot that really saves...' 可知它是一个机器人。

questions.prompt: Do you want to have Robbie in your family? Why? (Select the most appropriate answer statement)
options: Yes, I do. Because Robbie can help do a lot of housework, such as cleaning and cooking, saving us time and energy to relax.
No, I don't. Because a robot cannot do any cleaning work and it can be extremely dangerous at home.
Yes, I do. Because it can help me finish my school English exams directly.
No, I don't. Because having a robot will make the house freezing cold.
questions.translation: 你想让你家里有一个罗比吗？为什么？
questions.explanation: A选项观点积极、理由全面，完美契合标准参考答案给出的模版句式。
